task: Full Site Audit — Zelia Vance Engine
context: This is a multi-brand e-commerce engine built on Astro 5, Tailwind CSS v4, Alpine.js, and Snipcart v3. The engine was recently refactored from a monolithic BaseLayout to a 3-tier layout system (BaseLayout → EngineLayout → BrandEngine/AffiliateEngine). Some regressions may have been introduced. Your job is to audit the ENTIRE codebase and produce a detailed report. Do NOT make any code changes. Report only.
instructions: 1. LAYOUT AUDIT: Read src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro, src/layouts/EngineLayout.astro, src/layouts/CheckoutLayout.astro, and src/components/features/BrandEngine.astro. Verify the script loading order is correct (Snipcart config must come BEFORE the CDN JS). Check that snipcart-events.ts and snipcart-init.ts are both imported. Check that snipcart.css is loaded. Report any missing scripts, wrong import paths, or wrong load order.

2. PAGES AUDIT: Read every file in src/pages/. Check that ALL pages (except pages in src/pages/checkout/ and src/pages/api/) use <EngineLayout> as their layout wrapper, NOT BaseLayout directly. Report which pages are using the wrong layout.

3. COMPONENT AUDIT: Read src/components/ui/PopupModal.astro and src/scripts/behaviors/popup.ts. Verify the denylist prop is correctly typed, passed from site settings, and handled in the Alpine.js behavior. Verify that src/components/features/BrandEngine.astro correctly passes snipcart-init.ts and snipcart-events.ts scripts.

4. CONTENT SCHEMA AUDIT: Read src/content/config.ts. Check that the 'settings' collection schema includes: name, tagline, description, url, email, phone (optional), address (optional), social, announcement_bar, popup_modal (with denylist as array of strings), free_shipping_threshold, monetization (with show_ads boolean). Report any missing or mismatched fields versus what is in src/content/zelia-vance/settings/site.yml.

5. KEYSTATIC CONFIG AUDIT: Read keystatic.config.ts. Check that all collection paths use import.meta.env.PUBLIC_BRAND_ID (NOT process.env). Check that the products, blog, lookbooks collections and the settings singleton are correctly mapped. Report any TypeScript type errors or mismatched field types.

6. ENV AUDIT: Read .env.example. Verify all required variables are documented. Check that PUBLIC_BRAND_ID, PUBLIC_SITE_URL, PUBLIC_AFFILIATE, PUBLIC_SNIPCART_API_KEY, and IMAGE_GATEWAY_URL are present. Report any missing variable documentation.
7. AFFILIATE PLACEHOLDER AUDIT: Check that src/content/affiliate_zee/ exists with subfolders: blog/, settings/. Verify settings/site.yml exists and is valid YAML. Report structure.

8. PRODUCE A REPORT: Create a file called AUDIT-REPORT.md in the .plans/ folder. Structure it with sections for each audit area above. Use checkmarks (✅) for passing items and warnings (⚠️) for issues found. Be specific — include file paths and line numbers where issues exist. Do NOT fix anything. Report only.
